title: Looking Back at My First Semester

sentences.en: Mr. Pan asked us to write one long entry looking back at the whole first semester, so here is my honest attempt.
sentences.zh: 潘老師要我們寫一篇長長的回顧，看看整個第一學期，所以這就是我誠實的嘗試。

sentences.en: Whenever I look back at my early entries, I notice how nervous and formal I sounded, almost like I was writing for a stranger.
sentences.zh: 每次回頭看我早期的日記，我都注意到自己聽起來又緊張又拘謹，好像是在寫給一個陌生人看的。

sentences.en: Now I write wherever I happen to be, whether that is my desk, the couch, or even the bus, and it feels much more natural.
sentences.zh: 現在我不管人在哪都能寫，不管是我的書桌、沙發，甚至公車上，感覺自然多了。

sentences.en: I used to think a diary needed a strict daily schedule, but I seldom write every single day, and that turns out to be perfectly fine.
sentences.zh: 我以前以為日記需要嚴格的每日排程，但我很少每天寫，而事實證明這樣完全沒問題。

sentences.en: Some entries came from real difficulty, like the day our classroom fish died, and some came from silly, easy topics, like William's robot costume.
sentences.zh: 有些篇章來自真正的難過，像是教室的魚過世那天，也有些來自輕鬆好寫的主題，像是 William 的機器人裝。

sentences.en: Bernice, whom I barely knew at the start of the semester, is now somewhat close to being my best friend, which I never expected in September.
sentences.zh: 學期一開始我幾乎不認識的 Bernice，現在已經算是我頗親近的好朋友了，這是我九月的時候完全沒料到的。

sentences.en: William, whose favorite topic changes constantly, has appeared in almost every single one of my entries, either causing trouble or being surprisingly wise.
sentences.zh: 興趣話題一直在變的 William，幾乎出現在我每一篇日記裡，不是在闖禍，就是意外地很有智慧。

sentences.en: Throughout the semester, Sophia's rules stayed exactly the same, though I have learned to follow them without complaining quite so much.
sentences.zh: 整個學期 Sophia 的規矩都完全沒變，不過我已經學會遵守它們，不再抱怨得那麼多了。

sentences.en: I used to think school was mainly about tests and grades; now I understand it is also about the small, ordinary moments between classes.
sentences.zh: 我以前以為上學主要是為了考試跟成績；現在我明白它也包含了下課之間那些平凡的小片刻。

sentences.en: Some classmates I barely spoke to at first became fellow club members, and a few remain simply names I recognize but do not truly know.
sentences.zh: 有些一開始我幾乎沒講過話的同學，後來變成了社團夥伴，也有些依然只是我認得名字但不真正認識的人。

sentences.en: My handwriting is somewhat better than it was in September, though it is far from what I would call neat or careful.
sentences.zh: 我的字比九月的時候好一點了，不過離我認為整齊或工整還差得遠。

sentences.en: I no longer think "diary" is a shameful word, though I will admit it took the entire semester to feel that way, not just one lucky day.
sentences.zh: 我不再覺得「日記」是個可恥的字了，不過我得承認，這種感覺是花了整整一學期才建立起來的，不是靠某一天幸運就達成的。

sentences.en: Perhaps the biggest change is simple: I now actually want to write, rather than seeing it as required homework I must finish.
sentences.zh: 或許最大的改變很簡單：我現在是真的想寫，而不是把它當成必須完成的功課。

sentences.en: Until this semester, I had no real reason to pay attention to ordinary days; now I understand they are, quite honestly, most of my life.
sentences.zh: 在這學期之前，我完全沒有理由去注意平凡的日子；現在我明白，老實說，它們就是我人生的大部分。

sentences.en: So, to whoever reads this notebook someday, near or far: this was my very first semester of keeping a diary, and I regret none of it.
sentences.zh: 所以，未來不管誰讀到這本筆記本，不管在近處還是遠處：這是我寫日記的第一個學期，而我一點都不後悔。

quiz.question: How does Coco describe her early diary entries compared to now?
options: They sounded nervous and formal, almost like writing to a stranger.
They were exactly the same as now.
They were much longer than now.
She refuses to look back at them.

quiz.question: What does Coco say is the biggest change in how she feels about writing?
options: She now actually wants to write, rather than seeing it as required homework.
She only writes when forced to.
She writes only about William now.

quiz.question: How does Coco feel about the word "diary" now, compared to the start of the semester?
options: She no longer thinks it is a shameful word.
She still thinks it sounds childish.
She has stopped using the word entirely.
She thinks it should be renamed "journal."
